title: शुक्र कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री शुक्र कवचम् ॥

॥ श्री गणेशाय नमः ॥

अस्य श्रीशुक्रकवचस्य भरद्वाज ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। शुक्रो देवता।
शुक्रप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

शुक्रः शिरो मे रक्षतु ललाटं दैत्यपूजितः।
नेत्रे मे कवितागुरुः कर्णौ भार्गवपुङ्गवः॥ १॥

नासां मे दैत्यगुरवः मुखं मे काव्यनायकः।
जिह्वां मे भृगुवंशजः कण्ठं मे शुक्रदैवतम्॥ २॥

भुजौ मे दानवाचार्यः करौ रक्षतु भार्गवः।
हृदयं मे सिताचार्यः कुक्षिं रक्षतु शुक्रकः॥ ३॥

नाभिं रक्षतु मे शुक्रो मध्यं पातु ग्रहेश्वरः।
कटिं पातु महातेजा ऊरू रक्षतु वै भृगुः॥ ४॥

जानुनी मे ज्ञानवान्पातु जङ्घे रक्षतु शोभनः।
पादौ मे रक्षतु सदा शुक्रः सर्वसुखप्रदः॥ ५॥

शुक्रवारे विशेषेण कवचं शुक्रदैवतम्।
यः पठेच्छ्रद्धया युक्तः सर्वसौख्यमवाप्नुयात्॥ ६॥

सौन्दर्यं लभते नित्यं धनधान्यसमृद्धिमान्।
कलासु कौशलं याति दाम्पत्यसुखमाप्नुयात्॥ ७॥

॥ इति श्री शुक्र कवचम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Shukra Kavacham ||

|| Shri Ganeshaya Namah ||

Asya Shri Shukra Kavachasya Bharadvaja Rishih |
Anushtup Chhandah | Shukro Devata |
Shukra Prityarthe Jape Viniyogah ||

Shukrah Shiro Me Rakshatu Lalatam Daitya Pujitah |
Netre Me Kavita Guruh Karnau Bhargava Pungavah || 1 ||

Nasam Me Daitya Guravah Mukham Me Kavya Nayakah |
Jihvam Me Bhrigu Vamshajah Kantham Me Shukra Daivatam || 2 ||

Bhujau Me Danavacharyah Karau Rakshatu Bhargavah |
Hridayam Me Sitacharyah Kukshim Rakshatu Shukrakah || 3 ||

Nabhim Rakshatu Me Shukro Madhyam Patu Graheshvarah |
Katim Patu Mahateja Uru Rakshatu Vai Bhriguh || 4 ||

Januni Me Jnanavan Patu Janghe Rakshatu Shobhanah |
Padau Me Rakshatu Sada Shukrah Sarva Sukhapradah || 5 ||

Shukravare Visheshena Kavacham Shukra Daivatam |
Yah Pathech Chraddhaya Yuktah Sarva Saukhyam Avapnuyat || 6 ||

Saundaryam Labhate Nityam Dhana Dhanya Samriddhiman |
Kalasu Kaushalam Yati Dampatya Sukham Apnuyat || 7 ||

|| Iti Shri Shukra Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
शुक्र मेरे मस्तक की, दैत्यपूजित ललाट की, कविगुरु नेत्रों की, भार्गवपुंगव कानों की रक्षा करें।

दैत्यगुरु नासिका की, काव्यनायक मुख की, भृगुवंशज जिह्वा की, शुक्र देवता कण्ठ की रक्षा करें।

दानवाचार्य भुजाओं की, भार्गव हाथों की, सिताचार्य हृदय की, शुक्रक उदर की रक्षा करें।

शुक्र नाभि की, ग्रहेश्वर मध्य की, महातेजा कटि की, भृगु ऊरुओं की रक्षा करें। ज्ञानवान घुटनों की, शोभन जंघाओं की, सर्वसुखप्रद शुक्र पैरों की रक्षा करें।

शुक्रवार को विशेष रूप से इस कवच का श्रद्धापूर्वक पाठ करने से सम्पूर्ण सुख प्राप्त होता है। सौन्दर्य, धन-धान्य की समृद्धि, कलाओं में कौशल और दाम्पत्य सुख प्राप्त होता है।
benefits: Protective kavach for Venus (Shukra) related planetary issues
Enhances beauty, charm and artistic abilities
Invokes wealth, prosperity and material comforts
Blesses marital harmony and conjugal happiness
Recite on Friday for maximum benefit from Venus
description: The Shukra Kavacham, known in Sanskrit as शुक्र कवचम्, is a divine armor prayer for protection in praise of the Nine Planets (Navagraha). Attributed to Graha Stotra Sangraha, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to protective kavach for Venus (Shukra) related planetary issues, bless marital harmony and conjugal happiness, and recite on Friday for maximum benefit from Venus. This sacred text is ideally recited on Friday (Shukravar) and during Shukra Jayanti for enhanced blessings. Comprising 7 verses, it can be completed in approximately 3 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Shukra Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shukra Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Venus protection kavach for beauty, wealth, marital happiness and artistic skill.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shukra Kavacham and how is it traditionally classified?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shukra Kavacham is a seven-verse Sanskrit armour-prayer (kavacha) attributed to Maharishi Bharadvaja and dedicated to Shukra (Venus), the bright morning and evening star treated as one of the nine planetary deities (navagraha) in Vedic astrology. Composed in the Anushtup metre, the kavacham systematically envelops the chanter's body part by body part — head, forehead, eyes, ears, nose, mouth, tongue, throat, arms, hands, heart, abdomen, navel, hips, thighs, knees, calves and feet — in Shukra's protective grace. It is preserved within the Graha Stotra Sangraha collection of navagraha-kavachas and circulates widely in jyotisha digests for daily Friday recitation. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Shukra Kavacham; Graha Stotra Sangraha.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Shukracharya in puranic tradition and why is he the daityaguru?
seoFaqs.answer: Shukracharya (literally the seer of Shukra) is the son of Maharishi Bhrigu (hence Bhargava and Bhrigu-vamshaja in the kavach) and the guru of the asuras and danavas — Daitya-pujita, Daitya-guru, Danavacharya — counterpart to Brihaspati who is guru of the devas. Puranic tradition (Bhagavata Purana, Vishnu Purana, Mahabharata) credits Shukracharya with possessing the Sanjivani Vidya — the secret science of resurrection — which he obtained through severe Shiva tapasya, enabling him to revive slain asuras in the deva-asura wars. He is also called Kavi (poet, hence Kavya-nayaka) and Sita-acharya (the white-bodied teacher), and presides over Friday, the planet Venus, and the rasas of Vrishabha and Tula. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 9.18; Mahabharata Adi Parva; Wikipedia Shukra.)

seoFaqs.question: What does Shukra govern in Vedic astrology?
seoFaqs.answer: Shukra (Venus) is the karaka (significator) for marriage, love, romance, conjugal happiness, sexual fulfilment, beauty (saundarya), comfort, luxury (bhoga), wealth (especially through arts and entertainment), vehicles (vahana), white substances, sweet things, perfumes, jewellery (especially diamonds), the arts (dance, music, drama), poetry, fashion, cosmetics, and the female reproductive system. He owns the rasas Vrishabha (Taurus) and Tula (Libra), is exalted in Meena (Pisces) and debilitated in Kanya (Virgo). His friends are Shani and Budha; enemies Surya and Chandra. Shukra mahadasha lasts twenty years and typically brings material prosperity, marriage and artistic success when well-placed. (Sources: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra; Saravali; AstroSage Shukra.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Shukra Kavacham be chanted for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: Friday (Shukravar) is the day prescribed for Shukra in jyotisha — the kavach itself states Shukravare visheshena kavacham Shukra-daivatam (verse 6) — especially the Shukra Hora (Venus hour) on Friday and the Shukla Paksha Fridays. Devotees facing Shukra mahadasha (the twenty-year Vimshottari period), Shukra antardasha within larger dashas, Shukra retrograde in transit, marital difficulties, delays in marriage attributed to Venus affliction, problems in conjugal life, or those born under Bharani, Purvaphalguni or Purvashadha nakshatras (Shukra nakshatras) are advised to recite eleven, twenty-one or one hundred and eight times. It is also recited during Shukra Jayanti, Akshaya Tritiya, and Lakshmi puja occasions. (Sources: Drikpanchang Shukravar; AstroSage Shukra remedies.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the phalashruti of Shukra Kavacham promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti (verses 6-7) declares that the devotee who recites this Shukra Kavacham with shraddha on Friday obtains sarva-saukhya (complete happiness), saundaryam labhate nityam (lasting beauty), dhana-dhanya-samriddhi (wealth and grain prosperity), kalasu kaushalam yati (skill in the arts), and dampatya-sukham apnuyat (conjugal happiness). For unmarried persons it removes delays in marriage and brings auspicious matches; for married couples it restores harmony and intimacy; for artists, performers, designers and beauty professionals it grants creative inspiration and commercial success; for women it grants Lakshmi-like prosperity. The kavach is particularly powerful when paired with offerings of white flowers, sweets, sugar, white cloth and white sandalwood. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi phalashruti; AstroSage Shukra remedies.)

seoFaqs.question: What gemstones, mantras and rituals accompany Shukra Kavacham recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Jyotisha pairs the kavacham with the beej mantra Om Shum Shukraya Namah (or the longer Om Dram Drim Draum Sah Shukraya Namah), japa with a sphatika (crystal) or rudraksha mala of 108 beads, and offerings of white flowers (jasmine, white lotus, mogra), white sweets (kheer, makhana, white pedha), curd, ghee, sugar, white cloth, white sandalwood paste, perfumes, fragrant incense and rose water. Wearing a flawless natural diamond (heera) — or its substitutes white sapphire (shweta-pukhraj) or zircon — set in white gold or silver on the middle or ring finger of the right hand after vedic prana-pratishtha is the prescribed gemstone remedy for serious Shukra affliction. Donations on Fridays — white items, sweets, perfume, silver — to brahmins, young women and girls are recommended. (Sources: Drikpanchang Friday vrat; AstroSage Shukra remedies.)
